item.title: Origin of Australian term 'willy-willy' links back to Pilbara First Nations
item.description: It's not unusual to see willy-willies kicking up dust across WA, but did you know the name of the weather phenomenon comes from WA's north-west?

item.title: Iron Maiden: Burning Ambition documentary a fan-focused look at metal icons
item.description: The Iron Maiden documentary is a 50-year history of heavy metal's flashiest band as told by their biggest fans.

item.title: Cats spoil Magpies' party as concussion floors Moore, Suns down Saints in Darwin
item.description: A huge collision sees Darcy Moore concussed as Geelong takes control against Collingwood at the MCG as Gold Coast downs St Kilda in Darwin with star defender Cal Wilkie injured.

item.title: Record beef exports to the United States as prices soar ahead of 'grilling season'
item.description: The United States is importing more beef from Australia as its own supplies dwindle and demand soars.

item.title: CSIRO to persist with cutting up to 350 jobs despite extra $387.4 million in federal funding
item.description: Australia's national science agency CSIRO will persist with job cuts to up to 350 roles despite an extra $387.4 million in funding announced from the federal government.

item.title: Ponga stunned as Queensland left 'shattered' by Dearden Origin blow
item.description: Newcastle Knights captain Kalyn Ponga was left stunned after his side's big win over St George Illawarra, being told Queensland will be without halfback Tom Dearden for State of Origin.

item.title: Maroons lose Tom Dearden to injury ahead of 2026 State of Origin series against Blues
item.description: Queensland selectors have been forced to cope with the news one of their best playmakers will miss up to eight weeks with his ankle injury.

item.title: NRL explains Parramatta Eels three-point-play ruling to defeat North Queensland Cowboys
item.description: The NRL says an unusual referee call, which allowed the Parramatta Eels to attempt a penalty goal after scoring in golden-point, was the correct decision.

item.title: Sussan Ley says Liberals need to 'change or die' as One Nation wins Farrer by-election — as it happened
item.description: The ABC projects David Farley has won the Farrer by-election, becoming the first One Nation candidate ever elected to the House of Representatives.

item.title: Aboriginal elders return human remains to Rottnest Island in cultural burial
item.description: The remains of an Aboriginal person discovered during early construction works last month is returned to Rottnest Island for a cultural burial.

item.title: Melbourne City books A-League Women's grand final spot after stunning Shelby McMahon goal
item.description: Shelby McMahon fires Melbourne City into the A-League Women's grand final, with her side downing Melbourne Victory 2-0 on aggregate.

item.title: One Nation scores historic win in Farrer by-election
item.description: One Nation scores an historic win in the Farrer by-election, marking the first time the party has won a Lower House seat in federal parliament.

item.title: Quiz: Think you've got a way with words? Guy Montgomery's here with your Sunday spelling quiz
item.description: Ready to hone your homophones and verbalise your vowels? Let's get spelling!

item.title: Diagnosed as depression, caused by work. Who pays the cost of burnout?
item.description: Burnout is everywhere — in resignation letters, therapy sessions and late-night emails. Yet the debate over what it is reveals a bigger question: Should workplaces bear responsibility for the damage modern work can cause?

item.title: Cheat Sheet: How Big Brother embedded into Australian culture
item.description: Imported to Australia 25 years ago, Big Brother introduced the country to the concept of 24-hour monitoring as entertainment.

item.title: Mother at the NGV explores artists' experiences of motherhood throughout history
item.description: Mother at Melbourne's NGV offers contrasting depictions of motherhood, from Toscani's Madonna and Child to First Nations weaving.

item.title: Iran is trying to formalise its control over the Strait of Hormuz, but there are some obvious problems
item.description: Amid a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, the IRGC has launched a maritime authority for the Strait of Hormuz. The Persian Gulf Strait Authority says ships need to email their application for passage. So, we sent them a request.

item.title: Councils to get federal funds for roads, pipes and wires in housing push
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers will reveal details of the $2 billion fund in Tuesday's federal budget, declaring the cash injection will add an extra 65,000 homes over a decade.

item.title: Confounding bankers' assumptions, the RBA's post-COVID rate hikes caused Australians to work more, not less
item.description: The International Monetary Fund found that thousands of Australians took on a second or third job when the RBA hiked rates rapidly in 2022 and 2023. It expected the opposite.

item.title: From stealing cars to acting: How Tropfest founder John Polson channelled his chaotic past
item.description: John Polson was a troubled teen when a sliding doors moment led him into the world of acting. He went on to create Tropfest, one of the world's biggest short film festivals, changing his life and the Australian industry.

item.title: Farrer presents One Nation as a genuine electoral threat rather than just a protest
item.description: The result in Farrer has made One Nation's attempts to become the natural home for conservative Australians look less like a protest and more like a genuine electoral threat.

item.title: Ancient white wine variety reducing water bills and instilling hope for struggling Australian industry
item.description: In an Australian first, two South Australian wine grape growers are trialling an ancient variety from Cyprus that is reducing water consumption and reconnecting them to their Mediterranean roots.

item.title: How everyone is making money, and what it means for their tax
item.description: In Australia, the way people earn their money affects the rate of tax they pay — and top earners have a very different income mix from everyone else.

item.title: Historic Melbourne mansion Labassa 'not just a building, it's a story'
item.description: From post-war Jewish refugees, who arrived with nothing but hope, to the bohemian artists and performers who took over in the 1970s, historic Melbourne mansion Labassa has more stories than rooms.

item.title: Alice Springs town camp renters sick of begging for 'bandaids', just want safe homes
item.description: Conditions in town camps in Alice Springs are under intense scrutiny, but the residents are caught in a tangled web of bureaucracy with little transparency on the money being spent and who exactly is responsible for what.

item.title: Players across the country to protest gender-based violence and call for better treatment in sport
item.description: Players from various codes across the country will protest gender-based violence and call for greater accountability from clubs when instances of mistreatment arise.

item.title: Watson's Tree Frog Breeding Program
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Hope for endangered Watson's tree frog as tadpoles released into wild
item.description: Scientists hope the successful captive breeding and release of the endangered Watson's tree frog in Victoria's far east will help aid the species' survival, after its habitat was decimated during the Black Summer bushfires.

item.title: How Nigel Farage moved from the political fringe to contender for UK prime minister
item.description: Nigel Farage has his eye on the prime minister's job himself and, if Reform can sustain this level of support at a general election, the keys to Downing Street may fall to him.

item.title: The Drover's Wife becomes an opera in a new collaboration between Leah Purcell and George Palmer
item.description: Leah Purcell’s The Drover’s Wife is being transformed into an opera with music by George Palmer and starring soprano Nina Korbe.

item.title: Scientists call for offshore carcass disposal after 'disastrous' whale removal operation at Bellambi Boat Ramp
item.description: Marine scientists want to see whale carcasses towed offshore and disposed of out to sea in a process they say is safer, cheaper and more ecologically friendly.

item.title: Inquiry into military sexual violence a time for Defence to reckon with past, but only if it removes victims' gag orders
item.description: The impending military sexual violence inquiry is a time for Defence to "reckon with" its past. But advocates say it will be half-baked unless defence lifts gag orders on victims ahead of hearings.

item.title: Ongoing breakdowns hamper mobile breast screening service in Tasmania, but government says fix on the way
item.description: Angie is almost the age her mum was when she died from breast cancer, so getting a screening is important to her. But access to Tasmania's screening service has been difficult.

item.title: Restored cider mill crushes first apples in more than 100 years
item.description: A recently restored, hand-powered mill that revolutionised cider-making has crushed its first batch of apples in more than 100 years.

item.title: Kira Puru gained fame as a musician, so why have they pivoted to acting in Bad Company?
item.description: Kira Puru is known for their triple j Hottest 100-charting energetic pop music, but this year the musician has roles in not one, but two of the biggest Australian TV shows. What's behind the pivot?

item.title: Iran will 'definitely compete in FIFA World Cup', seeks assurances over visas for key players
item.description: Iran demands assurances that team members who served in the Revolutionary Guard, including skipper Mehdi Taremi, will be able to get visas for the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Steel City students to compete in hydrogen racing world title in Switzerland
item.description: A group of Whyalla high school students is heading to Switzerland to race a remote-control hydrogen-powered car that they designed and engineered.

item.title: Gas tax revenue revised up in budget aiming to 'fix' broken housing system
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers says the tax on offshore gas projects has raised more revenue than expected this year, as he prepares to hand down a budget focused on fixing a "broken" housing market and tax system.

item.title: Notorious Unit 18 empty while prisoners sleep on floor amid overcrowding crisis
item.description: The notorious Unit 18, a makeshift youth detention facility within the adult Casuarina Prison, has sat empty for at least a week while other WA prisons are bursting at the seams.

item.title: Pauline Hanson lashes 'arrogant' critics who say One Nation can't go mainstream
item.description: Pauline Hanson says the major parties "will lose" because of an "arrogant" belief that One Nation is not able to become a mainstream force, following the party's first lower house win this century.

item.title: Home destroyed by fire rebuilt as an Earthship made of tyres, bottles and cans
item.description: Dee-Ann Kelly lost her home to fire in 2024, now she's rebuilding an Earthship, made from recycled materials in an effort to make her home more fire resilient.

item.title: Police locate fail-to-stop driver after fatal crash in Glen Aplin
item.description: A car rolled and smashed into a power pole, killing its elderly passenger, after swerving to avoid a car driving the wrong way on the New England Highway in southern Queensland.

item.title: Love story: Love when you don't know how long you've got
item.description: My 14-year-old daughter Pip has Williams syndrome. The future is uncertain and full of heartache, but my love for my baby is not.

item.title: Rockhampton sisters raise $70,000 for Leukaemia Foundation in honour of cherished mum
item.description: Elli Wust was pregnant with her first child when she found out her mum had a rare blood cancer. Now she has two children who never got to meet their grandmother, who died at age 58.

item.title: FBI investigating recent deaths and disappearances of multiple US scientists
item.description: The scientists supposedly all had access to sensitive government research, and the nature of their work has led to speculation that their cases might all be connected.

item.title: Crows surge past Tigers with strong second half, Dees down Eagles
item.description: Adelaide breaks the shackles after the main break to down a plucky Richmond, as the Demons have a comfortable win over the Eagles who lose a key defender to another concussion.

item.title: Storm snap record losing run as Tigers coach laments 'negative smell'
item.description: The Melbourne Storm are winners for the first time since round two, snapping a club-record losing streak with a big win over the Wests Tigers.

item.title: Sunwater chair calls on Queensland government to dump troubled dam rebuild
item.description: In a letter addressed to the water minister, Sunwater chair Jeff Seeney said the decision to rebuild the dam lacked "any rigour" or "professional credibility".

item.title: Injury concern for women's world number one Aryna Sabalenka ahead of French Open
item.description: Women's tennis world number one Aryna Sabalenka has a fortnight to recover from injury before the French Open after her loss in Rome.

item.title: Woman charged after alleged antisemitic slur at children's netball game in Sydney
item.description: Jewish community leaders say a woman's alleged slur at an under-12 girls netball game in Sydney reflects a troubling pattern playing out on sporting fields.

item.title: Wellington Phoenix progress to A-League Women's grand final with victory over Brisbane Roar
item.description: Wellington Phoenix has become the first side from New Zealand to make an A-League grand final after inching past Brisbane Roar in their semifinal.

item.title: This week in history quiz, May 11: How much did Michael Jordan's signed Air Nike 1s sell for?
item.description: From the Tudor period right through to the 21st century, test your knowledge of the past with this week's history quiz.

item.title: Split Enz: Tim Finn on the band’s history, legacy and reunion shows
item.description: Tim Finn on the past, the present and the possible future of Split Enz.

item.title: Fashion designers are experimenting with AI, but not everyone is a fan
item.description: As Australian Fashion Week gets underway in Sydney, local and international designers debate the merits and detractions of using AI in the industry.

item.title: Federal government has missed one idea to get housing back on track: build more homes
item.description: If it's serious about improving intergenerational fairness and housing affordability in this week's budget, perhaps the federal government should just build some houses.

item.title: When is the 2026–27 federal budget? Here's what you need to know before tomorrow
item.description: Budget night features a lot of political talk and policy wonkiness, but the government's plan will impact your financial situation in one way or another, so it's quite important. Here's what you need to know.

item.title: One Nation didn't just win a protest vote in Farrer — Australian politics is deeply disrupted
item.description: Drafting Barnaby Joyce created a permission structure that has allowed conservative or swing voters to support One Nation: if the bloke who was deputy prime minister thinks the Coalition has lost its way, something in the system must be broken.

item.title: Aged care patients stranded in hospitals prompt calls for government action
item.description: The number of aged care patients stuck in hospitals has jumped by 35 per cent in less than a year, fuelling fresh demands for the federal government to stem the crisis.

item.title: Inside the course targeting young tradies with get-rich schemes
item.description: "Escape the 9-5" content is blowing up on social media, but people who have gotten caught up in it warn of the costs.

item.title: Australian Wedgetail plane gave NATO 'critical' information during Russian incursion over Estonia
item.description: A European official says they'd be "very happy" if Australia deployed the Wedgetail aircraft again, after it played an important role as NATO responded to an incursion of Russian jets over Estonia.

item.title: How Australian gas giants are using Singapore to reduce taxes
item.description: Amid renewed debate over whether Australia is getting enough tax from its gas exports, an investigation by the ABC shows how resource giants use shell companies to shift profit.

item.title: AI recruited to speed up home approvals, in budget set to reshape housing market
item.description: Artificial intelligence will be recruited to speed up assessments of housing and energy projects waiting for environmental approval, as the government races to build more homes and ward off a key frustration turning voters away from the major parties.

item.title: Confined 17 exhibition showcases art of incarcerated First Nations women
item.description: A day after her 18th birthday, Flick Chafer-Smith hit rock bottom when she was sent to jail. She found salvation through art.

item.title: Anzac Day Bledisloe Cup Test between Wallabies and All Blacks can be a major win
item.description: Australian rugby lacks a marquee event that captures the national imagination each year, but this could change if a bold Bledisloe Cup proposal gets approval.

item.title: Antarctic ship Nuyina's refuelling saga unresolved as deadline looms, documents show
item.description: A costly refuelling saga affecting Australia's Antarctic icebreaker might not be resolved for another three years under most options, internal government documents reveal.

item.title: Authorities evacuate passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Queensland weather to bring widespread 'nuisance' showers and 'unseasonable' rain in the west
item.description: The weather bureau says cooler morning temperatures in Queensland have been put on pause, with scattered showers expected across the week.

item.title: Bondi royal commission to hear challenge to antisemitism definition, quiz UK experts
item.description: A royal commission sparked by the Bondi terror attack is this week expected to hear from groups who challenge the commission's accepted definition of antisemitism, as well as expert witnesses from the United Kingdom.

item.title: Australians returning home from hantavirus cruise ship to quarantine in Perth — as it happened
item.description: Australians returning home from hantavirus cruise ship will quarantine in Perth.

item.title: NSW Health agency to pay $50k in damages to worker in disability discrimination case
item.description: An admin officer has been awarded more than $50,000 in compensation after a tribunal found a NSW Health agency discriminated against her due to her disability.

item.title: Mary Fowler's Manchester City beats Sam Kerr's Chelsea in FA Cup semifinal, Dario Vidošić's Brighton beats Liverpool
item.description: Mary Fowler's goal kick-started Manchester City's FA Cup comeback to down Sam Kerr's Chelsea and set up a final against Brighton, led by former Socceroo Dario Vidošić.

item.title: This is what experts say you should not include in your resume
item.description: Here's what three experts recommend you don't put in your resume for the best chance of securing an interview or landing a role.

item.title: As EV sales soar a motoring body says 'time is now' for charging expansion
item.description: South Australia's RAA says now is the time to expand EV charging infrastructure to ensure it meets growing demand, which represented about one-sixth of new vehicle sales in April.

item.title: Arsenal beats West Ham thanks to VAR call with massive implications at top and bottom of EPL ladder
item.description: Another late goal and a controversial VAR call have helped Arsenal stay in control of the Premier League title race, while delivering another blow to relegation-threatened West Ham United.

item.title: Australian flags flown upside down as protesters try to 'make Victoria great again'
item.description: Unhappy Victorians are flying upside-down Australian flags in political protest, but what exactly does it mean, and what do their neighbours think?

item.title: Two mums on having babies in their 40s
item.description: Whether it's about overcoming health challenges, waiting to meet "the right person" or wanting to be financially stable, Australian women are increasingly having children later in life.

item.title: Knox Family brings country, rock and stories to Sydney Opera House for 'Generations and Dynasties'
item.description: Uncle Roger Knox, aka the Black Elvis, will perform a collection of his hit songs and a few yarns from his life at the Sydney Opera House, sharing the stage with his son and two grandsons.

item.title: Lucas Herbert secures maiden LIV win despite final-round jitters
item.description: Australian Lucas Herbert is off to the US Open after scoring a nerve-shredding four-shot victory at LIV Golf Virginia in Washington DC.

item.title: Ambulance Victoria condemns assault on pregnant paramedic in Melbourne
item.description: A pregnant paramedic has been assaulted in Melbourne less than a fortnight after the stabbing of a colleague, the union says.

item.title: Court to rule if Andrew Forrest will have to compensate the Yindjibarndi for mining their land
item.description: Tomorrow will mark a historic day for an outback WA community as the Federal Court decides how much compensation it is due after Andrew Forrest's FMG mined its land without permission.

item.title: India calls on citizens to stop buying gold as Asia scrambles for energy amid Iran war
item.description: India's prime minister has urged Indians to work from home, avoid travel and stop buying gold, as the Iran War continues to send energy costs soaring.

item.title: Traffic camera captures 'spectacular' meteor breaking up over Tasmania
item.description: Tasmanians across the state reported seeing the bright lights of a meteor last night. Astronomer Martin George says the spectacle was typical of a natural object burning up in the atmosphere.

item.title: Afghanistan's women's cricket team urges ICC to replicate FIFA recognition
item.description: Afghanistan's exiled women cricketers are urging the International Cricket Council (ICC) to follow FIFA's lead in recognising them as a legitimate national team.

item.title: Trees razed as North Adelaide golf course redevelopment gets underway
item.description: Workers have begun cutting down trees at the North Adelaide Golf Course as part of a controversial redevelopment at the site.

item.title: Victoria to fund free meningococcal B vaccines for Year 10 students
item.description: The government will fund a $9 million program to vaccinate students against meningococcal B from January 1 next year.

item.title: Western Australia will fail to achieve net-zero by 2050 on current trajectory, Woodside-funded report warns
item.description: Woodside-commissioned modelling suggests WA is not on track to reach net zero by 2050, even if its massive Browse gas project proceeds, unless renewable energy is rolled out at unprecedented speed.

item.title: Jewish teen subjected to antisemitic slurs on Minecraft, royal commission told
item.description: The Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion hears a 15-year-old was subjected to antisemitic slurs while playing the popular video game with students from a Perth school.

item.title: Dermot Kennedy on his busking beginnings
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Police investigate reports of hundreds of pregnant sheep stolen from Great Southern farm
item.description: A farmer in Western Australia's Great Southern region has lost about $100,000 after someone stole hundreds of his pregnant sheep.

item.title: Keysborough Secondary College teacher Kim Ramchen jailed for knife attack on principal
item.description: The court heard IT teacher Kim Ramchen "snapped" and stabbed Keysborough Secondary College principal Aaron Sykes while children were still in class.

item.title: New trial ordered for Qld pilot Leslie Woodall over crash that killed tourist
item.description: Leslie Woodall had been flying three tourists from Agnes Water in central Queensland when the engine failed and the plane crashed, killing a woman.

item.title: Australian Defence Force Academy cadet charged after allegedly using hidden bathroom camera to film women
item.description: An Australian Defence Force Academy cadet who allegedly used a camera hidden in a soap dispenser to film female cadets in the bathroom has been granted bail in the ACT Magistrates Court.

item.title: Queensland's beleaguered DNA lab clears backlog of hundreds of sexual assault kits, director says
item.description: Forensic Science Queensland was the subject of two commissions of inquiry, over failures in testing and cultural issues which may have led to miscarriages of justice.

item.title: Son of drug smuggler denies helping dad with Newcastle cocaine importation scheme
item.description: A man accused of helping his father carry out a drug importation operation in Newcastle tells a jury his dad led a double life.

item.title: ICAC investigating whether ex-City of Parramatta Council CEO subverted protocols to hire friends
item.description: ICAC is investigating allegations the former CEO of City of Parramatta Council subverted hiring practices to fill job vacancies with friends.

item.title: Sunwater CEO Glenn Stockton resigns amid Paradise Dam controversy
item.description: The ABC understands Sunwater CEO Glenn Stockton resigned on Friday after almost six years in the position.

item.title: Hitchhiking northern quoll released back into wild in central Queensland
item.description: Members of the public spotted the marsupial fleeing the wheel arch of a truck in Brisbane and alerted authorities.

item.title: First Nations man dies in custody at Lotus Glen Correctional Centre in Far North Queensland
item.description: The death is not being treated as suspicious and a report is being prepared for the coroner. Fourteen Indigenous people have died in Australian prisons this year.

item.title: Hasrat Gill stumbled upon the T20 World Cup on TV one day. It changed her life forever
item.description: State and WBBL cricketer Hasrat Gill has risen through the ranks of youth Australian cricket. She is one of many South Asian teenagers flocking to the sport.

item.title: Cafe-worthy breakfast and brunch recipes for relaxed mornings
item.description: From shakshuka to French toast, you can enjoy an indulgent breakfast or brunch at home with one of these recipes.

item.title: NDIS provider avoids jail for defrauding scheme in bid to keep business 'afloat'
item.description: An Adelaide man has avoided jail for making a series of fraudulent claims with the NDIS in a bid to help the struggling business he was working for survive.

item.title: Maree Mavis Crabtree found not guilty of murdering son Jonathan with spiked fruit smoothie
item.description: After hearing evidence from more than 60 witnesses across five weeks, a jury retired to deliberate on Thursday afternoon and returned a verdict on Monday.

item.title: Deadline set by cybercriminal group looms as some institutions regain Canvas access
item.description: Australian universities and education departments have begun regaining access to online learning platform Canvas, after being caught up in a breach last week. However, the deadline set by the hackers continues to count down.

item.title: Son allegedly murdered mother in Adelaide Hills after years of 'resentment', court hears
item.description: Crafers West man Bo Sebastian De-Simone, 40, is standing trial in South Australia's Supreme Court, charged with the murder of his mother Linda Simon, 62, in 2022.

item.title: Aboriginal man expresses remorse after avoiding jail for vandalising culturally sensitive site
item.description: An Aboriginal man has pleaded guilty to vandalising a culturally significant site in Victoria's Alpine National Park.

item.title: Canberra to Sydney rail link gets $100 million upgrade to improve train speeds
item.description: The federal, ACT and NSW governments will spend $100 million upgrading the Canberra to Sydney rail link in a bid to reduce the train trip to under four hours.

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship passengers to quarantine in Perth
item.description: Australian passengers who were on board the hantavirus-hit cruise ship will be ordered to quarantine in Perth for at least three weeks.

item.title: Josh Rachele urges fans to 'choose kindness' after receiving abusive message online
item.description: Adelaide Crows footballer Josh Rachele has called out fan behaviour after receiving an abusive message online amid an ongoing conversation on mental health in the AFL.

item.title: Drink, drug driver David Berry jailed for more than a decade over motorcyclist death
item.description: A County Court judge has sentenced a drink, drug driver to 11 years' jail after the man hit and killed a motorcyclist while behind the wheel of a stolen ute in Central Victoria.

item.title: Calls about One Nation's electoral chances in Western Sydney 'premature', analyst says
item.description: One Nation's Barnaby Joyce says potential candidates for Western Sydney were first and second-generation Australians, but an analyst calls him a "great media performer".

item.title: Boy rescued after being trapped under train at North Melbourne Railway Station
item.description: A year 7 student is in a serious but stable condition in hospital after being hit by a train and becoming trapped for up to an hour.

item.title: University of Newcastle academic with terminal cancer risks job to speak out about 'unsafe' workloads
item.description: The University of Newcastle has been found to be worst in class when it comes to staff wellbeing. Now, an academic with terminal cancer is speaking out about it because others fear they cannot.

item.title: Alleged Russian spies Kira and Igor Korolev to face fresh espionage conspiracy charge
item.description: Australian citizens Kira and Igor Korolev were charged in 2024, accused of attempting to gather and send state secrets to Russia.

item.title: Dua Lipa sues Samsung for using her face to sell TVs
item.description: The lawsuit alleges Samsung featured a copyrighted image of the pop star on the front of cardboard boxes containing televisions for sale.

item.title: Man sentenced to life in prison for Palm Island murder of Günther Franco Messmer
item.description: Four and a half years after a respected council worker was murdered by his housemate, his killer has been sentenced.

item.title: Clinton Gordon Sutcliffe found guilty of murder of Dale McKenna in Qld car crash
item.description: Three years ago, Dale Aaron McKenna was pursued down a dark country road at 180km/h, dying after his ute rolled. Now, the man chasing him has been convicted of his murder.

item.title: Magistrate questions strength of case against teen accused of setting alight Cairns early learning centre
item.description: A magistrate has told the court a "strong case couldn't be confidently asserted" against a 19-year-old accused of setting fire to a Cairns early learning centre.

item.title: Rain to consolidate in the north and southeast this weekend
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Monday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Penny Smith sets world record at trap shooting World Cup in Kazakhstan
item.description: Paris Olympics bronze medallist Penny Smith sets a new trap shooting world record with a flawless performance in the World Cup final in Kazakhstan.

item.title: ICC confirms arrest warrant for Philippine Senator Roland Dela Rosa over war on drugs
item.description: Video footage displayed during the Senate session showed Philippine senator Roland Dela Rosa running away from Philippine agents as the International Criminal Court confirmed an arrest warrant had been issued.

item.title: Quadriplegic man invents way to pilot hands-free
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Hadley's shock return 10 weeks post-baby
item.description: Paige Hadley made a shock return to the Super Netball league less than two months after giving birth to her baby boy. Despite some commentary online questioning her priorities, the midcourter is more determined than ever to prove women can pursue multiple dreams.

item.title: Academics speak out about conditions at University of Newcastle
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Deputy Liberal leader Jane Hume promises new Coalition after Farrer by-election loss
item.description: Deputy Liberal leader Jane Hume is promising a new day for a Coalition that has lost its way, but whether the party can recover from the wreckage of Farrer remains to be seen.

item.title: Man charged with White House correspondents' dinner shooting pleads not guilty
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, has been charged with attempting to assassinate US president Donald Trump at the April dinner.

item.title: US, Iran reach stalemate in war negotiations dashing hopes of Strait of Hormuz reopening
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he's aiming for "complete victory" in Iran as the US and Iran reach an impasse in negotiations.

item.title: Keir Starmer vows to prove 'doubters' wrong as UK Labour MPs demand his exit
item.description: The British prime minister is fighting to fend off a growing threat to his leadership after a disastrous set of local election results last week.

item.title: The caring burden for single, childless women
item.description: Single women over 45 without children are much more likely to be spending time caring for family members who are aging, have a disability or chronic illness. And it's not just costing them financially.

item.title: How Mortal Kombat II brought Hollywood to the Gold Coast
item.description: The $110 million Mortal Kombat sequel is the next in a laundry list of productions putting Queensland on the radar for international films.

item.title: Why Chalmers will rein in the property free-for-all in the federal budget
item.description: After being rejected twice by an electorate that didn't want to give up lucrative tax arrangements on property, the Albanese government has sniffed the breeze and decided the time is ripe for change.

item.title: What will be in tonight's federal budget? Here are the major measures we know about so far
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers is unveiling his fifth budget tonight, and housing for younger Australians will be a centrepiece.

item.title: More or Less quiz: Guess where your money goes, and what it gets spent on
item.description: A new quiz format? In this economy? Celebrate budget day with our More or Less challenge.

item.title: Vanuatu's cabinet approves new version of Nakamal Agreement with Australia
item.description: The ABC can reveal that after months of difficult negotiations, the Nakamal Agreement has been approved by Vanuatu's cabinet but omits key limitations on China's influence.

item.title: Cricket Australia hopes Big Bash League privatisation could make it one of sport's 'grand slams'
item.description: Cricket Australia chief executive Todd Greenberg says private investment in the Big Bash League is "inevitable" and waiting for too long could mean "we've missed the boat" as more competitions pop up around the world.

item.title: Trump and Xi's Beijing summit must confront the AI cold war
item.description: When Trump last visited China in 2017, artificial intelligence was not yet the centre of global power. Now it is.

item.title: Eurovision begins amid boycotts and massive police operation in Vienna
item.description: This week, the Eurovision Song Contest will burst onto the stage in Vienna, and onto screens around the globe, but behind the colour and campness, this year's competition is shaping up as one of the most politically charged in history.

item.title: Homes Tasmania might come and go before Felicity gets a rental
item.description: Felicity Hirst says she is not too picky about what kind of house she lives in; she just wants one. She has been on Tasmania's social housing list for three and a half years — as long as the state's housing authority has existed.

item.title: Study finds link between air pollution exposure and risk of ending up in hospital
item.description: A new study has found a clear link between air pollution exposure and the risk of ending up in hospital. It's a battle Julia Ovens knows only too well.

item.title: Efforts to secure buyer for Liberty Bell Bay smelter ongoing after window for offers closes
item.description: The window for non-binding offers to purchase for the Liberty Bell Bay smelter, in Tasmania's north, has officially closed, with a union delegation taking their cause to Canberra as workers anxiously await news.

item.title: Tonight's federal budget set to tackle housing inequity. Will it revive the Great Australian Dream for those priced out?
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers will hand down the federal budget tonight. It's expected to tackle housing inequity, but will it bring back the Great Australian Dream?

item.title: No conviction recorded for former Queensland Health employee who repeatedly beat puppy
item.description: A member of the public who reported the abuse said the sentence made it hard to believe animal suffering was taken seriously.

item.title: Queensland man pioneers app to fly drones using head movements
item.description: Ever since he was a young child, Christopher Hills has wanted to fly an aircraft. Now he has made that dream a reality.

item.title: Wife murderer Colin Arthur Case issued five-year parole period for sexual abuse of child
item.description: A man who stalked and shot his wife dead on an Adelaide street in 1992 and sexually abused a teenage girl after being released from jail has been handed a five-year parole term in South Australia's Supreme Court.

item.title: First north-west cloudband of 2026 to develop over Australia, delivering a broad band of rain
item.description: In a welcome twist of weather variability, the first north-west cloudband of 2026 will finally develop over Australia this week, delivering a broad band of rain.

item.title: Students criticise Adelaide University's communication over Canvas cybersecurity breach
item.description: Adelaide University says access to the cloud-based learning platform Canvas is back online after a data breach last week, but some students have criticised the institution's communication about the incident.

item.title: Legal Aid NT to consider more cuts amid funding shortfall, months after services slashed
item.description: One of two legal bodies responsible for representing vulnerable Territorians in court is considering making further cuts, six months after its services were previously slashed.

item.title: High Court to consider whether court of appeal could swap jury's murder verdict to manslaughter
item.description: The case of a Canberra man convicted over the death of his partner after she fell from the bonnet of his car will reach the High Court today, after the ACT Court of Appeal swapped the jury's verdict of murder to manslaughter.

item.title: Small robots used to help students consider careers in renewable energy
item.description: Students are using tiny, programmable robots to code in a bid to encourage them to consider careers in renewable energy due to predicted skill shortages.

item.title: Live: PM deflects questions on broken promises ahead of budget
item.description: The prime minister has deflected questions in Question Time about whether the federal government's budget breaks promises made at the last election. Follow live.

item.title: Apple's Tim Cook and Tesla's Elon Musk among top US CEOs to accompany Trump to China
item.description: According to a list shared by a White House official, speaking on condition of anonymity, Elon Musk and Tim Cook will be accompanied by 15 other chief executive officers when Donald Trump visits China.

item.title: Alexei Popyrin brushed aside by world number one Jannik Sinner at Italian Open
item.description: Alexei Popyrin has been outclassed in Rome, beaten 6-2 6-0 by world number one Jannik Sinner in 65 one-sided minutes in the Italian Open third round.

item.title: Health minister invited to spend week with regional NDIS family
item.description: Regional families are anxiously waiting to see the impact of recently announced changes to the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), especially for those with autism.

item.title: AFL needs to do more to encourage Indigenous players as marked decline continues at top level
item.description: The AFL is considering a range of measures to boost the number of Indigenous players in the league following a significant decline in recent seasons.

item.title: Volunteer gives central Queensland hospice residents free haircuts
item.description: Discussing how people want to be treated at the end of their life can be hard, but experts say planning can bring dignity and comfort.

item.title: More than 70 UK Labour MPs call for Sir Keir Starmer's resignation — as it happened
item.description: Six ministerial aides have stepped down and more than 70 UK Labour MPs have publicly called for Sir Keir Starmer's resignation.

item.title: Live: ASX slips as budget in focus while DroneShield probed by ASIC
item.description: Australian shares have slid while DroneShield is being investigated by ASIC over disclosures and share trading. Follow the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Bullsbrook Centre for National Resilience to be used to quarantine passengers from hantavirus ship
item.description: Nestled away in Perth's outer fringes this 500-bed facility was built during the COVID-19 pandemic as a quarantine centre, but sat idle for years. Now, a different deadly virus will see it used for its intended purpose.

item.title: Coach Michael Voss resigns from AFL strugglers Carlton, Mick Malthouse says premiership winning player will be relieved
item.description: Michael Voss has left Carlton, resigning with immediate effect following a run of seven consecutive AFL defeats, with another former Carlton boss Mick Malthouse saying the Brisbane premiership-winning player will be relieved to be out of the hot seat.

item.title: Captain of Hantavirus-hit cruise ship praises crew and guests
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship's captain praises crew and guests
item.description: The MV Hondius's captain has praised the patience and kindness of his crew and guests as they disembark.

item.title: Darwin chemical recycler prepares to make diesel from gas condensate
item.description: Global Resource Recovery NT says production of the first diesel made from Central Australian gas condensate will leave Darwin in July.

item.title: Alleged driver charged over deaths of two girls in Viveash crash
item.description: Police charge a 24-year-old man with dangerous driving causing death, a fortnight after two girls died from the injuries they suffered when their car smashed into a tree in Viveash.

item.title: Man charged over allegedly robbing cash transit vehicle with weapon in Hervey Bay
item.description: Police have charged a man who allegedly stole a significant amount of money from a cash-in-transit vehicle while armed with a weapon in Hervey Bay.

item.title: Australian paratrooper Warrant Officer Class Two Lachlan Muddle killed during training exercise at Jervis Bay
item.description: Warrant Officer Class Two Lachlan Muddle, a highly qualified special forces sniper and military free-fall parachutist, has died after a mid-air collision with another paratrooper during an army training exercise.

item.title: Gout Gout named for 200m, 4x400m relay at World Athletics U20 Championships
item.description: Gout Gout is set to take part in the 200m and 4x400m relay at the Under-20 World Athletics Championships in August.

item.title: Man arrested after allegedly setting another on fire in Warrawong unit
item.description: Emergency service crews were called to an apartment complex in the Wollongong suburb of Warrawong overnight after a 25-year-old man was set on fire.

item.title: Jessica Pegula putting family experience in NFL and NHL to use in tennis prize money protest
item.description: Jessica Pegula is putting her family experience of the NFL and NHL to use in tennis players' fight against the French Open for increased prize money.

item.title: Prosecutors drop domestic violence charges against Lockhart River mayor Wayne Butcher
item.description: The long-serving mayor of Lockhart River on eastern Cape York was facing seven domestic violence charges but the case against him won't proceed.

item.title: Joanne Ciconte double-podium on Japan debut, Summer Rintoule makes GT4 Australia history
item.description: Australia's strong cohort of young female racing drivers has shone at home and abroad, with a former F1 Academy driver achieving consecutive podiums on her Japanese debut and history made in the GT4 Australia.

item.title: Sitting near the Strait of Hormuz, seafarers are in limbo as Iran war drags on
item.description: As a peace deal between the US and Iran remains elusive, low-wage seafarers are in limbo as they witness an unprecedented 21st-century battle.

item.title: CCTV footage of alleged armed robbery on Queensland's Fraser Coast
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australian government finalising plans to bring hantavirus cruise ship passengers home
item.description: Six passengers from a cruise ship at the centre of the deadly hantavirus outbreak are "in good health", as the Australian government remains hopeful it will finalise plans to repatriate them within 48 hours.

item.title: Prosecutors drop murder, manslaughter charges against Isla Bell's accused killer Marat Ganiev
item.description: Melbourne man Marat Ganiev, who was accused of killing 19-year-old Isla Bell and putting her remains in a fridge, was due to face a Supreme Court trial this month.

item.title: What giving up alcohol taught me about myself
item.description: What started out as abstinence during a pregnancy four years ago turned into a game with myself to see how long I could go without alcohol.

item.title: New laws to deter organised criminals recruiting young people introduced to NSW parliament
item.description: New offences and stronger punishments for the use of so-called kill cars and the recruitment of children by organised criminals have been introduced in the Parliament of NSW.

item.title: Carlton boss says Voss resignation proof club is in 'genuine transition'
item.description: Carlton CEO Graham Wright says the club will embark on a thorough process to determine the successor to Michael Voss.

item.title: Press Gallery exhibition at Old Parliament House explores media's role in political history
item.description: The new Press Gallery exhibition takes visitors inside the original working spaces at Old Parliament House where reporters worked between 1927 and 1988.

item.title: Racist attacks leave New Zealand's Indian community feeling 'hounded'
item.description: Indian communities across New Zealand say they are feeling "hounded" after a surge in racist incidents.

item.title: Fraught 24 hours ahead as Keir Starmer clings to UK prime ministership
item.description: UK cabinet will meet today, local time, amid growing calls for Keir Starmer to set out an exit plan.

item.title: Four more Australian National University council members quit after Julie Bishop's resignation as chancellor
item.description: The acting chancellor of the Australian National University confirms four more members of the university council have resigned, following last week's exit of former chancellor Julie Bishop.

item.title: Multiple sclerosis patient spends night at Adelaide hospital waiting for an access taxi
item.description: A multiple sclerosis patient says he waited three hours for an access cab, only to be told in the middle of the night one would not be available, forcing him to spend the night at an Adelaide emergency room.

item.title: Year 7 boy trapped under train at North Melbourne station had bag snagged by mirror, school says
item.description: The St Aloysius College student remains in a serious condition in hospital after the incident yesterday afternoon.

item.title: Jack Alex Cullen jailed for trafficking cocaine while in Australian Defence Force
item.description: The Supreme Court of Queensland hears Cullen, a former infantry soldier, was sourcing ounces of cocaine and selling it to fellow soldiers while in the Australian Army.

item.title: Canvas developer Instructure says 'agreement' reached with hackers
item.description: The company at the centre of a cyber attack involving online learning platform Canvas, which is used right across Australia, says it has reached an "agreement" with the hackers responsible.

item.title: Brisbane's council backflips on promise to introduce licences for landlords using Airbnb, Stayz
item.description: Brisbane City Council announces it will not go ahead with plans to regulate landlords' use of short-stay apps such as Airbnb and Stayz.

item.title: Queensland Deputy Premier Jarrod Bleijie uses parliamentary privilege to attack Labor MPs
item.description: It comes after more than a week of scrutiny on ministerial lovers Tim Mander and Amanda Camm.

item.title: Canberra teen charged with planning terrorist attack and transmitting violent extremist material
item.description: The 17-year-old appeared in the ACT Children's Court today charged with one count of planning or preparing for a terrorist act and one count of transmitting violent extremist material.

item.title: Bungled Spirit of Tasmania ferry replacement project has cost $717 million more than first forecast
item.description: The Spirit of Tasmania ferry replacement program has cost $717 million more than was forecast in the original business case, a parliamentary inquiry has heard.

item.title: Wildlife carers fear possums and birds will be victims of tree felling at North Adelaide golf course
item.description: Wildlife carers are concerned possums and birds will become casualties as trees are felled for the government's $45 million North Adelaide golf course redevelopment.

item.title: Research highlights antisemitism impact on women, royal commission told
item.description: About 80 per cent of Jewish women in Australia have experienced antisemitism personally or against an immediate family member in the past two years, the royal commission has heard.

item.title: Murdoch University promises improvements after viral video shows issues with wheelchair access
item.description: The former Murdoch University student's video highlights the difficulty wheelchair users have navigating the campus since stair lifts were removed in 2023.

item.title: Holmes à Court family-owned Heytesbury buys 11 live export ships in major deal with Vroon
item.description: Owned by the Holmes à Court family, Heytesbury runs 165,000 head of cattle in northern Australia and has just struck a deal with Vroon to buy its ships.

item.title: Tax reform to help 75,000 buyers into first homes, government says ahead of federal budget's release
item.description: In a political push to get ahead of criticism it has broken election promises, Labor is making a case for how wealth is taxed in Australia by promising the budget will open the housing market to younger buyers.

item.title: Government accused of 'burying' response to landmark gambling report with budget day release
item.description: A long-awaited response to an inquiry that called for a gambling advertising ban has been tabled on budget day, prompting accusations the government is trying to bury the document.

item.title: Queensland government says it will continue with Paradise Dam rebuild despite advice to ditch project
item.description: The board of Sunwater has written to the state government, labelling the rebuild of Paradise Dam "grossly disproportionate" and should be "no further considered".

item.title: Yindjibarndi people win $150m compensation after Andrew Forrest's Fortescue mines built without permission
item.description: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to pay $150 million to WA traditional owners after mining their land without permission.

item.title: Sydney cage fighter found not guilty of murder but guilty of manslaughter for killing best friend
item.description: Bradley Fletcher has been found not guilty of murder but guilty of manslaughter for killing his best friend during a backyard sparring session and leaving his body by a footpath.

item.title: Judge's social media posts at centre of Stephen Fane Noga sexual abuse verdict appeal
item.description: A former Hobart teacher sued by a former student over allegations of repeated sexual abuse has filed an appeal on the grounds of social media posts made by the judge presiding over his case.

item.title: Murderer sentenced to life in prison over death of 22yo Dale Aaron McKenna
item.description: A man is sentenced to life behind bars for the murder of 22-year-old Dale Aaron McKenna in Queensland's Western Downs.
